GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Alessandro Michele has designed the perfect Gucci Christmas gift", "It's an old-meets-new collection of bags to love.", "Trust Gucci's creative director Alessandro Michele to sprinkle a little magic dust over your wish list for Santa at precisely the right time. For the aptly titled Beloved range, Michele has re-imagined Gucci's most iconic bags made for treasuring forever - and we'll take one of each. The Italian-born luxury design house has a storied history with the humble handbag, since its founding in Florence almost a century ago. Originally makers of saddles and riding accessories, Gucci fast expanded to bags that garnered them cult status, as well as a legion of fans, including Elizabeth Taylor and Grace Kelly in the process. And it's no accident that some of their most recognisable handbags have remained in their collections to this day, albeit now with a signature Michele touch. As a house, Gucci have long masters at celebrating their rich heritage without slowing down its forward trajectory. The brand's iconic interlocked 'G' logo, made up of two 'G' initials, is an homage to founder Guccio Gucci. Gucci's famed use of canvas on their handbags came about because of a scarcity of leather during the 1930s, and still features on their most-carried styles today. This includes the Jackie, also on offer now in leather iterations. For the latest Beloved collection, Michele's update of the Jackie 1961 comes in summer-fresh shades of baby blue and blush pink, to blend seamlessly with a lightweight breezy summer wardrobe. For those who prefer their purse to be just for the essentials, the style has been rendered in mini size - and matching colours. Michele's creative reimaginings extend too to the Horsebit 1955, a refined and practical rework of the original polished design. Resurrected by Michele from the archives, it's versatility - it can be worn cross body with a longer strap, or over the shoulder - evidences the celebration of individual style that is key to the modern vision of the Italian house. With simple lines and an emphasis on the gold hardware (the horsebit hardware, a nod to the label's equestrian roots), it has a minimalist, unisex aesthetic affording it instant heirloom status. If you've been particularly good this year, consider the matching Horsebit loafers to match. For something current - and made for a party dress pairing - enter the colourful GG Marmont. With a chain strap, and recognisable metal detailing, it finds good company with the Dionysus. Both bags, made for slinging over the shoulder after hours, tap the singular brand of freethinking creativity with bohemian leanings that is Michele's calling card. First introduced by Gucci for autumn/winter '15/'16, the Dionysus is destined for the same cult status afforded to the house icons that have transcended decades."
